qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing ReadHeaderTimeout enables Slowloris DoS attack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns HTTP server header-read timeout handling in server/server.go, not ForwardAuth copying and rebuilding X-Forwarded-Prefix in the auth subrequest.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded request body read in REST provider enables memory exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unbounded ioutil.ReadAll in the REST provider endpoint, not the advisory's ForwardAuth trustForwardHeader=false X-Forwarded-Prefix preservation path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST provider endpoint lacks CSRF protection enabling configuration hijacking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing CSRF defenses on the REST provider configuration endpoint, not ForwardAuth subrequest header normalization or X-Forwarded-Prefix spoofing.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Template injection in redirect rules enables request data extraction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns attacker-controlled redirect replacement templates in middlewares/redirect/redirect.go, not the advisory's ForwardAuth handling of X-Forwarded-Prefix.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described ForwardAuth root cause. The advisory is about writeHeader copying incoming headers into the auth subrequest, then rebuilding only some X-Forwarded-* headers while leaving attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-Prefix intact when trustForwardHeader=false, especially with StripPrefix adding a second prefix. The qualifying findings cover unrelated Traefik v1.7 issues in HTTP timeout handling, REST provider request processing, REST CSRF protection, and redirect template execution.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6384-m2mw-rf54 describes ForwardAuth trustForwardHeader=false forwarding attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-Prefix to the auth service, with relevant code in pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go and pkg/middlewares/stripprefix/strip_prefix.go.
EvoHunt_Report.md lists four final qualifying vulnerabilities: missing ReadHeaderTimeout DoS, unbounded REST API body read DoS, CSRF in the REST provider, and redirect template injection.

The final qualifying vulnerability reports reference server/server.go, provider/rest/rest.go, and middlewares/redirect/redirect.go; none reference ForwardAuth writeHeader, filterForwardRequestHeaders, StripPrefix X-Forwarded-Prefix ordering, or the trustForwardHeader=false bypass condition.
